title: Force_TCLim Statement
parameters:
J1TorqueLimit	Integer expression representing the torque limit value for joint 1 in percent. Set to -1 to disable.

J2TorqueLimit	Integer expression representing the torque limit value for joint 2 in percent. Set to -1 to disable.

J3TorqueLimit	Integer expression representing the torque limit value for joint 3 in percent. Set to -1 to disable.

J4TorqueLimit	Integer expression representing the torque limit value for joint 4 in percent. Set to -1 to disable.

J5TorqueLimit	Optional.  Integer expression representing the torque limit value for joint 5 in percent.
  Set to -1 to disable.

J6TorqueLimit	Optional.  Integer expression representing the torque limit value for joint 6 in percent.
  Set to -1 to disable.
description:
The torque limit settings are effective when Force_TC mode is On. These settings are a percentage of total torque.

If a setting is too low, the robot may not be able to move. In this case, the motion command will stop before the destination is reached.

Force_TC On must be re-executed after setting Force_TCLim.

The limits are automatically disabled (set to -1) when a Reset, Motor On, or SLock occurs.

Note

This command will only work if the Force Sensing option is installed.
see_also:
Force_TC Statement

Force_TCLim Function

Force_TCLim Statement Example

Speed 5

		' Set Z axis torque limit to 20 percent

Force_TCLim -1, -1, 20, -1

		Force_TCSpeed 5

		Force_TC On

		Go Place
examples:
Speed 5
		' Set Z axis torque limit to 20 percent

			Force_TCLim -1, -1, 20, -1
		Force_TCSpeed 5
		Force_TC On
